title: Multi-Column Sort + featuredFirst for Accommodation Search

tasks.title: Add SortFieldSchema and SortField type in packages/schemas/src/common/pagination.schema.ts
Extend BaseSearchSchema with sorts (max 5) and featuredFirst
tasks.description:
Add the two new optional fields to the shared base search schema. Keep legacy sortBy/sortOrder for backward compat.

File: `packages/schemas/src/common/pagination.schema.ts`

Change BaseSearchSchema to:
```ts
export const BaseSearchSchema = z.object({
    page: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
    pageSize: z.number().int().positive().max(100).default(10),
    sortBy: z.string().optional(),
    sortOrder: z.enum(['asc', 'desc']).default('asc').optional(),
    sorts: z
        .array(SortFieldSchema)
        .max(5, { message: 'zodError.common.sort.maxFields' })
        .optional(),
    featuredFirst: z.boolean().optional(),
    q: z.string().optional()
});
```

Notes:
- Use `{ message: 'zodError.common.sort.maxFields' }` (legacy shim, matches codebase convention — see spec pass #5).
- Do NOT switch call-sites to `{ error }` (cross-cutting scope).

Tests: covered by T-009.

subtasks.title: Add sorts array with .max(5) and i18n key

tasks.title: Add common.sort.maxFields i18n key to es/en/pt validation locales
tasks.description:
Add the translation key used by the new Zod `.max(5)` error.

Files:
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/validation.json`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/validation.json`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/validation.json`

Add under an existing or new `common.sort` namespace:
- es: `No se pueden especificar más de 5 campos de ordenamiento`
- en: `A maximum of 5 sort fields is allowed`
- pt: `Um máximo de 5 campos de ordenação é permitido`

IMPORTANT: if a `common` namespace already exists in each locale, add `sort.maxFields` inside it rather than creating a duplicate top-level key. Keep valid JSON (double-quoted keys, trailing commas forbidden).

Tests: not testable in isolation — transform verified client-side in T-009.

tasks.description:
Add HTTP-coerced versions of the new fields.

Update `HttpSortingSchema` to include `sorts` and `featuredFirst`. Use the inline `.transform(...)` for `sorts` documented in the spec (parse `field:order,field:order`, drop empty, .slice(0,5)). Use `createBooleanQueryParam(...)` for `featuredFirst` — NOT `z.coerce.boolean()`.

Attach OpenAPI metadata via `.openapi({ example: 'averageRating:desc,name:asc' })` on `sorts` (project convention; do NOT use `.meta()`).

All new fields MUST have `.describe(...)` metadata. Keep existing `sortBy`/`sortOrder` unchanged (just add `.describe(...)` if missing).

Tests: covered by T-008.

tasks.title: Unit tests for HttpSortingSchema.sorts CSV transform (edge case table)
tasks.description:
Cover every row of the spec's Edge-case Table for sorts parsing.

File: add or extend a test file alongside `packages/schemas/src/api/http/base-http.schema.ts`.

Cases to assert:
- `averageRating:desc,name:asc` → 2 entries parsed in order
- `name` → `[{field:'name',order:'asc'}]` (missing order defaults asc)
- `name:invalid` → `[{field:'name',order:'asc'}]` (unknown order → asc)
- `:desc` → `[]` (empty field dropped)
- `` (empty) → `[]`
- 6 entries → only first 5 kept (`.slice(0,5)`)
- Duplicate field names `name:asc,name:desc` → both preserved

Also assert `featuredFirst`:
- `'true'` → true
- `'false'` → false
- `'truthy'` → fails validation (not an accepted literal)

Use AAA. Follow existing test style in the package.

subtasks.title: Tests for sorts CSV transform happy path
Tests for sorts edge cases (empty, unknown order, truncation)
Tests for featuredFirst strict boolean coercion

- 6 entries fail with issue `code: 'too_big'`, `origin: 'array'`, `message: 'zodError.common.sort.maxFields'`
- Zod v4 issue shape: use the literal string `'too_big'` (do NOT import `ZodIssueCode` enum in new tests)

File: alongside pagination.schema.ts test file or new sibling.

tasks.title: Rewrite orderBy in AccommodationModel.search() (multi-sort + featuredFirst + tiebreaker)
tasks.description:
Replace the single-column orderBy block at ~line 227 with the multi-sort + featuredFirst + stable-tiebreaker logic defined in the spec (see 'Model implementation').

Rules in order:
1. If `params.featuredFirst`: push `desc(accommodations.isFeatured)`.
2. Compute `rawSortFields = params.sorts ?? legacyFallback`, where `legacyFallback` = `[{field: sortBy, order: sortOrder ?? 'asc'}]` only if `sortBy` is set AND NOT (`featuredFirst && sortBy === 'isFeatured'`).
3. Filter out any `{field: 'isFeatured'}` entries when `featuredFirst === true` (dedup).
4. For each remaining sort: look up the column on `accommodations`; if valid, push `buildSortExpr(...)`.
5. Always append `desc(accommodations.id)` as stable tiebreaker.

INVOKE: `.orderBy(...orderBy)` — spread (select builder is variadic). Do NOT pass the bare array.

Tests: covered by T-013.

subtasks.title: Implement multi-sort + legacy fallback with dedup

tasks.title: Rewrite orderBy in AccommodationModel.searchWithRelations() (RQB findMany — bare array)
tasks.description:
Apply the same multi-sort + featuredFirst + tiebreaker logic to `searchWithRelations()` at ~line 374.

CRITICAL: this method uses `db.query.accommodations.findMany({ ... orderBy, ... })` (Relational Query Builder). Pass the orderBy array as a BARE array property, NOT spread. `...orderBy` inside an object literal is a SyntaxError.

Reuse `NUMERIC_NULLABLE_FIELDS` + `buildSortExpr` (T-010) so there is no duplication.

Tests: covered by T-014.

subtasks.title: Implement featuredFirst + sorts + dedup + fallback + tiebreaker
Ensure findMany uses bare array orderBy (no spread)

tasks.title: Unit tests for AccommodationModel.search() orderBy construction
tasks.description:
Integration-style test against a seeded test DB (follow patterns already used in `packages/db/test` or `packages/service-core/test`).

Cases to cover:
- `featuredFirst: true` only → featured rows first; tiebreaker present
- `sorts: [{field:'averageRating',order:'desc'}]` only → desc by rating then id desc
- `featuredFirst: true` + `sorts: [{field:'isFeatured',order:'asc'}, {field:'name',order:'asc'}]` → isFeatured entry deduplicated; result ordered featured DESC, name ASC, id DESC
- Legacy fallback: `sortBy: 'name', sortOrder: 'asc'` → name asc + id desc
- Legacy-path dedup: `featuredFirst: true, sortBy: 'isFeatured'` → ORDER BY is_featured DESC, id DESC (no duplicate)
- Empty/undefined → `ORDER BY id DESC` only
- 5 entries boundary → all applied
- NULLS LAST: seed a null-averageRating row; assert it appears last with `sorts: [{field:'averageRating',order:'desc'}]`

subtasks.title: Test dedup of isFeatured in sorts when featuredFirst=true
Test empty input → id DESC only
Test NULLS LAST on averageRating desc

tasks.title: Unit tests for AccommodationModel.searchWithRelations() orderBy construction
tasks.description:
Mirror T-013 but against `searchWithRelations()`. Keep the matrix tight: featuredFirst-only, sorts-only, dedup case, legacy fallback, empty → id DESC, NULLS LAST check.

Do not re-test every edge from T-013 if the helper path is identical — skip the 5-entry boundary and rely on T-013 for it. But DO cover the RQB-specific code path (findMany) once to prove the bare-array property works end-to-end.

tasks.title: Add sanitizeSorts() helper in apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/public/list.ts
Wire sanitizeSorts + forced featuredFirst:true in public list route; update header JSDoc
tasks.description:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/public/list.ts`

In the handler (inside the `accommodationService.search(actor, {...})` call), add:
```ts
sorts: sanitizeSorts(domainParams.sorts),
featuredFirst: true,
```

`featuredFirst: true` is FORCED regardless of client input — the client cannot opt out. This is a product rule enforced server-side.

Also update the file-header JSDoc (lines 1-17) to mention `sorts`, `featuredFirst` (forced), and the `features` filter.

Tests: covered by T-019/T-020.

tasks.title: Unit tests for sanitizeSorts()
tasks.description:
Cases:
- undefined input → undefined
- empty array input → undefined (collapse rule)
- all fields in whitelist → array returned unchanged
- some fields not whitelisted → filtered, remaining kept
- none whitelisted → undefined (collapse rule)

File: sibling test file to `list.ts` (follow existing pattern in `apps/api/test/unit/...` if one exists for this route).

tasks.title: Integration test: public list sorts=... returns featured first + stable pagination
tasks.description:
End-to-end test exercising `GET /api/v1/public/accommodations?sorts=averageRating:desc,name:asc&pageSize=N`.

Assertions:
(a) Featured items appear before non-featured.
(b) Within each group, results are ordered by averageRating DESC then name ASC.
(c) Paginating across two pages with the same query never repeats rows — proves the `id DESC` tiebreaker works.

File: extend `apps/api/test/integration/accommodation/list.test.ts` or add a sibling `list-sorts.test.ts`.

Use the existing test fixture/seed helpers.

subtasks.title: Seed featured + non-featured rows with tied sort keys
Assert pagination across two pages has no duplicates

tasks.title: Integration test: client featuredFirst=false cannot override server-forced true
tasks.description:
Assert that `GET /api/v1/public/accommodations?featuredFirst=false` STILL returns featured items first. The public route forces `featuredFirst: true`; no legitimate opt-out for clients.

File: same as T-019.

subtasks.title: Assert featured items still appear first

tasks.title: Quality gate: typecheck + lint across changed packages
tasks.description:
Run:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/schemas typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/db typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @hospeda/api typecheck`
- `pnpm lint` (root)

All must be green with no new errors/warnings.

tasks.title: Quality gate: full test run across changed packages
tasks.description:
Run:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/schemas test`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/db test`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core test`
- `pnpm --filter @hospeda/api test`

All must be green. No existing tests regressed.

subtasks.title: Verify no regressions in existing suites
